criteriaCoverage.evidence: login-workspace-switch real Chromium: switcher lists both WS, switching flips active; DB-level no-cross-bleed proven in workspace-scope.integration (wsA reads only SEG_A, after switch only SEG_B). WEAKNESS: browser spec doesn't assert WS_A data GONE in-DOM after switch (only select value).
role-visibility real Chromium: marketer nav hides billing/settings/admin; owner shows them. nav uses can() (§3A). accounting billing-only, system-admin console asserted in unit.

build-segment (live preview from real §8 compiler, WS_B excluded), broadcast-and-automation (real runBroadcast, validateAutomationDefinition), editor — pass real Chromium. handlers delegate to real cores, scoped by ctx.workspaceId.

dispatch: authorizer->contextFromAuthorizer->enforceRoute per route; router-role-enforcement.integration: marketer 403 billing/members/domain, accounting 403 POST segments, owner 403 /admin, no token 401. role-visibility e2e proves marketer 403 on /billing via in-browser fetch. apiClient assertNoWorkspaceIdInBody.

recommendations: Add integration test: WS_A token -> 404/403 on POST /broadcasts/{WS_B_broadcast_id}/send.
Strengthen login-workspace-switch e2e to assert WS_A's seeded data is absent in-DOM after switching to WS_B (surface a segment list).
criticalIssues: TENANT-ISOLATION GAP: sendBroadcast (services/local-api/src/handlers.ts:311) ignores ctx and calls runBroadcast(deps.broadcast, id) on ANY broadcast id with no check the broadcast belongs to ctx.workspaceId. runBroadcast loads workspace_id FROM the broadcast row, so a marketer in WS_A who knows a WS_B broadcast id could trigger WS_B's send (manage_content passes in their own ws). Real cross-tenant action vector; handler comment falsely claims the guard exists. Fix: verify broadcast row workspace_id == ctx.workspaceId before runBroadcast; add a cross-workspace-send-attempt test.
